item.title: Event 1: FCAJ Community Day
item.description:
FCAJ Community Day - May 2026

Event Information

Attributes Details
Event Name FCAJ Community Day - May
Time 09:00 AM - 12:00 PM, Saturday, May 23, 2026
Location Floor 26, Bitexco Financial Tower, 2 Hai Trieu St., Dist. 1, HCMC
Format Offline
Role Attendee / Intern
Organizer First Cloud AI Journey

Event Objectives

  • Update on the latest technology trends in Cloud Infrastructure (AWS) and Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI, LLMs).
  • Learn practical insights from top-tier experts on cost optimization, Multi-Agent architectures, and managing the stability of enterprise-grade AI models.
  • Expand professional networking within the engineer/developer community and members of the First Cloud AI Journey ecosystem.

List of Speakers / Organizers

  • Tinh Truong - Platform Engineer, GoTymeX
  • Pham Ng. Hai Anh - G-AsiaPacific Vietnam / AWS Community Builder
  • Nguyen Tuan Thinh - DevOps Engineer, First Cloud AI Journey
  • UTMorpho Team - Outstanding Participants at LotusHacks 2026
  • Duc Dao - Solution Architect, Cloud Kinetics
  • Vy Lam - Senior Business Systems Analyst, VPBank

Key Highlights

Non-Determinism of “Deterministic” LLM Settings (Speaker: Duc Dao - Cloud Kinetics)

  • Token Selection Mechanism: LLMs generate text token by token by converting logits into probabilities via a softmax function. Theoretically, setting Temperature = 0 triggers greedy decoding (argmax) — always selecting the single highest-probability token to guarantee 100% reproducible and deterministic outputs for the same prompt.
  • The Reality Gap: In practice, even with temp=0, LLMs can still return non-deterministic responses. This randomness does not stem from sampling, but rather from the underlying GPU architecture, floating-point calculation discrepancies, and various inference optimization algorithms utilized during hosting.
  • Impacts & Mitigation: Setting temp=0 often leads the model to get stuck in repetitive generation loops. A practical remedy is setting temp=0.1 as a “sweet spot”, which keeps the probability distribution extremely sharp (near-deterministic) while introducing a tiny stochastic nudge to prevent loop locks. Additionally, engineers should leverage higher repeat penalty values and fundamentally design downstream applications to tolerate variance from the start.

Enterprise-Grade Multi-Agent System: The Case of Startup Credit Scoring (Speaker: Vy Lam - VPBank)

  • The Structural Mismatch: Traditional banking credit scoring systems routinely fail to evaluate startups due to data misalignment. Traditional frameworks mandate 3+ years of audited financial statements, stable collateral, and predictable revenue patterns. In contrast, startup data is highly multi-dimensional, heavily unstructured, and volatile (6-18 months of operation, with core assets being IP, team composition, and market traction).
  • The Virtual Credit Committee Blueprint: To tackle multi-dimensional data, the architecture shifts from a Single Agent to a Multi-Agent paradigm. The system orchestrates multiple highly specialized agents: a Financial Agent (evaluating burn rate and runway), a Market Agent (analyzing TAM/SAM), a Team Agent (vetting founder backgrounds), and a Traction Agent. This distributed expertise delivers comprehensive insights and robust auditability that a single agent cannot match.
  • Enterprise Mindset & ROI: Transitioning AI from a working prototype to production-ready requires security and compliance to be implemented as a Day 1 mindset. The production architecture incorporates Cognito/OAuth2 for authentication, Bedrock Guardrails for content filtering, IAM least-privilege boundaries, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, all deployed via Terraform. This delivers a compelling business case with a 90%+ cost reduction and 95% faster processing speed.

Amazon Q Business: Leveraging Q Chat, Amazon Q Apps, custom web experiences, and Amazon Q in QuickSight to analyze data and automate operations using natural language.

Exploring CloudFront Foundation:

  • Deeply analyzed methods of optimizing static and dynamic content delivery across AWS’s edge network infrastructure.
  • Compared performance boundaries of CloudFront Functions (executing instantly at Edge Locations, suited for URL rewrites and basic header checks) vs. Lambda@Edge (running fully programmable, complex backend computational logic closer to regional endpoints).

LotusHacks Journey with UTMorpho:

  • Explored inspiring stories from developing and scaling the award-winning project at LotusHacks 2026.
  • Learned valuable teamwork skills, progress scheduling, and the rigorous transition process of converting an ambitious concept into a stable system operating on AWS.

Key Lessons Learned

Technical Domain

  • LLM Principles: Gained a deeper understanding of model non-determinism and the critical role of hyperparameter tuning in production environments.
  • Multi-Agent Architectures: Discovered how coordinating multiple specialized AI agents can systematically solve complex, domain-specific enterprise problems.
  • Security-First Integration: Realized that embedding security layers (Cognito, Bedrock Guardrails, least-privilege IAM) must be a Day 1 design priority.

Practical Skills

  • Learned how to optimize compute and cost margins when deploying AI systems at scale.
  • Discovered how to bridge academic theories with real-world enterprise applications in corporate finance.

Application to Work

  • Applying the Context Framework (Goals, Constraints, Criteria) to systematically interact with AI tools, dramatically improving daily task efficiency.
  • Strictly adhering to rigid Cloud IAM access controls and security boundary standards during upcoming enterprise internship assignments.

Personal Experience

Highlights

  • Strongly impressed by the Virtual Credit Committee architecture that elegantly solves the lack of historical startup financials.
  • The mathematical and hardware breakdown behind LLM repetitive loops clarified several major core engineering uncertainties.

Key Takeaways

  • The future does not pit Humans against AI, but rather humans who know how to engineer context against those who do not.
  • When pushing AI into production, stability, security boundaries, and data auditability hold far more weight than a flashy proof-of-concept demo.

Event Images

1 / 5

Conclusion

The May edition of FCAJ Community Day offered an all-encompassing perspective spanning from core cloud infrastructure up to intelligent enterprise logic layers. This pool of knowledge serves as a stellar foundation, setting up a proper architectural mindset for my internship and future career growth.

item.title: Week 1 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 1 Topic: AWS Account, Billing Management, AWS Support, and IAM

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 1

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Onboarding FCAJ & Reading regulations - WhatsApp / Internship Rules Understand communication channels, guidelines, and reporting requirements - Completed
Tue AWS Account Creation 000001 AWS Account / Billing Familiarize with AWS Free Tier, Management Console, and Billing Dashboard View Lab Completed
Wed Budgeting with AWS Budgets 000007 AWS Budgets Set up budgets, configure custom alerts, and monitor costs View Lab Completed
Thu AWS Support Portal & Cases 000009 AWS Support Understand Support Center, service tiers, and submit support cases View Lab Completed
Fri Access Management with AWS IAM 000002 AWS IAM Understand IAM Users, Groups, Roles, Policies, and least privilege access View Lab Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Create New AWS Account
Set Up AWS Budgets
AWS Support Interaction
Access Control with IAM

3. Topics Covered

  • AWS Account & Billing:
    • Successfully created an AWS Free Tier account and completed AWS Management Console login.
    • Verified account details, Free Tier status, active credits, and explored the Console Home interface.
  • AWS Budgets:
    • Explored methods to monitor and predict AWS resource consumption charges.
    • Created custom cost budgets with configured alert thresholds to prevent cost overruns.
  • AWS Support:
    • Accessed the AWS Support Center, reviewed default support plans, and understood how to open support tickets.
  • AWS IAM:
    • Acquired basic concepts of IAM Users, User Groups, Roles, and JSON Policies.
    • Understood how to enforce the Principle of Least Privilege across AWS resources.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Successfully registered and configured a personal AWS account.
  • Inspected account details and active promotional credits.
  • Implemented AWS Budgets to alert on exceeding usage thresholds.
  • Explored AWS Support options and logged a test inquiry.
  • Understood core access management concepts in IAM.
END OF WEEK 1

item.title: Event 2: FCAJ Community Day
item.description:
FCAJ Community Day - June 2026

Event Information

Attributes Details
Event Name FCAJ Community Day - June
Time 08:30 AM - 11:30 AM, Saturday, June 27, 2026
Location Bitexco Financial Tower, HCMC
Format Offline
Role Attendee / Intern
Organizer First Cloud AI Journey

Event Objectives

  • Update on emerging trends in Generative AI, Voice Agents, DevOps Agents, and cloud operations workflows on AWS.
  • Study how AI is applied to solve tangible business challenges such as conversational voice support, automated incident response, workforce planning, and secure enterprise data integration.
  • Deepen understanding of Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Nova Sonic, Model Context Protocol (MCP), and agentic architectures in building highly scalable, enterprise-ready systems.
  • Gain a practical, industry-centric perspective on executing AI solutions beyond simple static demos, focusing on real-world data and measurable business value.

Key Topics

  • Deep Response Engine: Bridging the gap between automated incident detection and resolution in modern cloud environments.
  • Voice Agents: Constructing highly conversational, human-like AI experiences at scale.
  • AWS DevOps Agent: Enhancing cloud operations, monitoring workflows, and driving down issue resolution cycles.
  • Amazon Q Business: Empowering enterprise productivity and supporting workforce optimization.
  • MCP Private Connection: Restricting AI interactions with internal enterprise tools and database models to secure private network pathways.

Key Highlights

Voice Agents: Building Human-Like AI Conversations at Scale (Speaker: Trung Vu - Revve AI)

This presentation was the highlight of the event for me. It did not merely showcase AI voice interfaces but provided a transparent look into the complex backend architectures and trade-offs required to operate Voice Agents at scale.

Requirements

  • An AWS Account with appropriate permissions
  • AWS CLI installed and configured

Estimated Cost

Permissions Required

🔐 List the IAM permissions needed.

item.title: Week 2 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 1 Topic: AWS Networking, IAM Roles, Route 53, and AWS CLI

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 2

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Discovering Amazon VPC 000003 VPC / Subnets / Route Tables / IGW Understand core networking components on AWS View Lab Completed
Tue Configuring Advanced VPC 000003 Public/Private Subnets / Security Groups Design and construct a multi-subnet network architecture View Lab Completed
Wed Granting Access with IAM Roles for EC2 000048 IAM Role / EC2 / IAM Policy Understand safe cross-service credentials delegation using IAM Roles View Lab Completed
Thu DNS Management with Amazon Route 53 000010 Route 53 / Hosted Zones / DNS Resolver Implement private DNS resolution and hybrid DNS architectures View Lab Completed
Fri Operations via AWS CLI 000011 AWS CLI / EC2 / IAM / S3 Learn command-line interactions for AWS resource management View Lab Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Amazon VPC Fundamental Configuration
1 / 4
Application Authorization via EC2 IAM Role
Hybrid DNS Resolving with Route 53
1 / 8
Command Execution with AWS CLI

3. Topics Covered

  • Amazon VPC Networking:
    • Explored fundamental networking blocks: VPCs, subnets, route tables, and Internet Gateways (IGW).
    • Provisioned a custom VPC, enabled DNS resolution, and enabled DNS hostnames.
    • Constructed routing tables to connect public subnets with the Internet Gateway.
    • Used the VPC Resource Map visualization tool to inspect topology.
  • IAM Roles for Compute:
    • Analyzed advantages of utilizing IAM Roles over hard-coding static Access Keys within EC2.
    • Provisioned an IAM Instance Profile granting S3 read access, and successfully attached it to an running EC2 instance.
  • Amazon Route 53 Private Resolver:
    • Studied Hybrid DNS architectures connecting on-premises servers with Cloud infrastructure.
    • Deployed lab resources using CloudFormation templates.
    • Provisioned Route 53 Inbound and Outbound Endpoints and defined custom resolver rules.
    • Validated correct bi-directional name resolution via nslookup command tests.
  • AWS CLI Administration:
    • Learned installation, profile setup, and interactive querying in terminal environments.
    • Verified target instance permissions fetched from the metadata service.
    • Executed basic AWS CLI operations on S3, SNS, IAM, and EC2, followed by clean-up scripts.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Designed a custom private networking boundary using Amazon VPC.
  • Set up public and private routing topologies.
  • Eliminated storage of static security credentials on VMs using IAM Roles.
  • Integrated a Hybrid DNS architecture using Route 53 Resolver endpoints.
  • Managed various AWS resources programmatically using the AWS CLI.
END OF WEEK 2

item.title: Week 3 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 1 Topic: Compute Services - EC2, Kiro CLI, Lightsail, and Load Balancing

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 3

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon VM Deployments on Amazon EC2 000004 Amazon EC2 / Sec Groups / Snapshots / AMI Launch Linux/Windows VMs, establish SSH/RDP connectivity, configure security groups View Lab Completed
Tue Local Development using Kiro CLI 000049 AWS Cloud9 / Kiro CLI / VS Code Terminal Transition from unavailable Cloud9 to Kiro CLI extension for local cloud execution View Lab Completed
Wed Simplified Apps via Amazon Lightsail 000045 Lightsail / Databases / Backups / Cost Control Deploy WordPress & PrestaShop web instances with managed databases View Lab Completed
Thu High-Availability Setup (ALB) 000006 Launch Template / ALB / Target Groups Prepare dynamic load balancing layers for compute endpoints View Lab Completed
Fri Auto Scaling Group Orchestration 000006 Auto Scaling / Scaling Policies / Testing Test elasticity thresholds, load stress scaling behavior, and clean up View Lab Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Multi-OS Deployments on Amazon EC2
1 / 4
Kiro CLI Integration on VS Code Terminal
1 / 3
Budget-Friendly Hosting with Amazon Lightsail
1 / 3
Deploy WordPress & PrestaShop CMS
1 / 10

3. Topics Covered

  • Amazon EC2 Lifecycle & Virtualization:
    • Launched Linux instances (SSH-access) and Windows Server instances (RDP-access).
    • Explored virtual storage blocks (EBS volumes), snapshot creations, and custom Amazon Machine Images (AMI).
    • Implemented Systems Manager Session Manager for RDP connections as a secure backup access path.
  • Kiro CLI Alternative IDE:
    • Adopted Kiro CLI (terminal-based workspace assistant) as AWS Cloud9 is restricted for newer student accounts.
    • Successfully authenticated the client and initialized a sample Node.js environment.
  • Amazon Lightsail Simplified Compute:
    • Launched a high-productivity single-node container/CMS instance for small projects.
    • Configured separate managed databases, assigned static IPs, and customized billing alarms.
  • Scalability & High Availability (ALB & ASG):
    • Created launch templates containing instance metadata and configurations.
    • Set up an Application Load Balancer (ALB) pointing traffic to defined Target Groups.
    • Configured an Auto Scaling Group (ASG) to dynamically maintain capacity based on traffic load.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Deployed and managed EC2 instances across different operating systems.
  • Configured AMI-based templates to easily replicate server setups.
  • Integrated Kiro CLI for terminal cloud development.
  • Launched functional WordPress and PrestaShop web applications on Lightsail.
  • Structured a highly-available, auto-scaling architecture behind an ALB.
END OF WEEK 3

item.title: Clean Up
item.description:
Clean Up

🧹 Always clean up AWS resources after completing a workshop to avoid unnecessary charges.

Resources to Delete

📝 List all resources created in this workshop that need to be deleted.

  1. (Resource 1 — service name + how to delete)
  2. (Resource 2 — service name + how to delete)
  3. (Resource 3 — service name + how to delete)

Verification

  • All resources deleted
  • Billing dashboard shows no unexpected charges
  • AWS account is clean

✅ Congratulations on completing the workshop!

item.title: Week 4 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 1 Topic: Storage, Databases, Monitoring, and Personal Project Launch

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 4

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Static Web Hosting on S3 000057 S3 / Static Site / CloudFront Configure bucket options, policy and ACL for hosting public web pages View Lab Completed
Tue Relational Database with RDS 000005 Amazon RDS / MySQL / Security Groups Launch a managed MySQL database in private subnets and connect EC2 clients View Lab Completed
Wed CloudWatch System Monitoring 000008 CloudWatch / Metrics / Alarms / SNS Set up metrics, CPU stress alarms, and email notifications View Lab Completed
Thu Personal Project Bootstrap: HTML/CSS Portfolio Personal Project S3 / HTML / CSS / Cloud Resume Draft personal portfolio markup and host the initial design on an S3 endpoint - In Progress

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Static Website Hosting on Amazon S3
1 / 3
Relational Database Setup with Amazon RDS
1 / 6
Metrics Monitoring with CloudWatch
1 / 7
Personal Project - Cloud Portfolio v1 on S3 View Evidence

3. Topics Covered

  • Amazon S3 Static Hosting:
    • Created S3 buckets, disabled block public access options, and configured bucket policies.
    • Uploaded assets and verified global access via the static website hosting endpoint.
  • Amazon RDS Private Databases:
    • Launched a managed MySQL RDS instance in private subnets using custom DB Subnet Groups.
    • Configured port 3306 Security Group policies to restrict database access only to authorized EC2 instances.
    • Deployed a backend Node.js server on EC2 and connected it to the database using an .env config file containing the RDS connection endpoint.
  • Amazon CloudWatch Auditing:
    • Monitored instance metrics and configured Simple Notification Service (SNS) alarms to send email alerts on high CPU usage.
  • Personal Project Initiation (AWS Cloud Portfolio):
    • Designed and structured an initial HTML/CSS portfolio template.
    • Uploaded the layout to S3 to prepare for future serverless backend integrations.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Hosted low-overhead static websites using Amazon S3 bucket features.
  • Securely separated database engines from the public internet using private subnets.
  • Established real-time alert notifications using CloudWatch and SNS.
  • Successfully initialized and hosted the frontend for the personal Cloud Portfolio.
END OF WEEK 4

item.title: Week 5 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 2 & 3 Topic: Security, Resource Governance, and Personal Portfolio Backend Setup

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 5

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon S3 Security Practices 000069 S3 / Encryption / HTTPS Enforce Apply bucket policies enforcing HTTPS transfers and KMS encryption View Lab Completed
Tue IAM Cost Control Governance 000064 IAM / EC2 Policy Limits Write restrictive IAM policies to enforce region and size policies View Lab Completed
Wed Tag-Based Resource Structuring 000027 Tagging / Resource Groups Organize AWS resources using Tag groups View Lab Completed
Thu Controlling EC2 access via Tags 000028 IAM / EC2 / Tag Permissions Write policies enforcing tag-matching to control EC2 actions View Lab Completed
Fri Personal Project: Backend Counter setup Personal Project DynamoDB / AWS Lambda / IAM Role Set up DynamoDB table and Lambda visitor counter function - In Progress

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Hardening Storage Security on Amazon S3
1 / 4
Resource Cost Control with IAM Policies
Organize Resources via Resource Groups View Evidence
Enforce Tag-Based IAM Access Controls View Evidence
Personal Project - Visitor Counter Backend
1 / 5

3. Topics Covered

  • S3 Secure Bucket Topologies:
    • Built test architectures using parameterized CloudFormation templates (modified EC2 sizes to t3.micro to maintain Free Tier limits).
    • Configured separate AWS CLI profiles (user1, user2) to test cross-boundary bucket accesses.
    • Wrote S3 Bucket Policies restricting HTTP access, accepting only TLS/HTTPS data transfers.
  • IAM Policy Governance:
    • Learned to prevent cost overruns using declarative IAM policies.
    • Configured IAM Policies using conditional operators (StringEquals, ArnEquals) that prevent spinning up high-cost server types, unauthorized EBS models, or working in unauthorized AWS Regions.
  • Tagging Strategy & Resource Groups:
    • Managed metadata boundaries on AWS using logical Key-Value tags.
    • Organized distributed resources into visual units using Tag-Based Resource Groups.
  • Personal Project Serverless Counter backend:
    • Created an Amazon DynamoDB NoSQL table (cloud-portfolio-table) using a partition key (id) to track site page visits.
    • Wrote a Node.js-based AWS Lambda function to increment and return database visitor logs.
    • Assigned a custom IAM execution role allowing the Lambda function to perform read/write queries on DynamoDB.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Standardized secure transit policies on object storage endpoints.
  • Enforced cost guardrails on IAM users.
  • Grouped cloud assets using structured tagging metadata.
  • Successfully initialized the backend counter lambda and verified logs in CloudWatch.
END OF WEEK 5

item.title: Week 6 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 2 & 3 Topic: Operations - Lambda Automation, Advanced Monitoring, SSM, IaC, and API Gateway

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 6

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Automated Server Shutdown & Alerts 000022 Lambda / EventBridge / Slack Webhook Build schedules to automatically shut down instances and notify via Slack View Lab Completed
Tue Advanced Dashboarding with Grafana 000029 CloudWatch / Grafana Deploy Grafana and configure IAM roles to fetch CloudWatch metrics View Lab Completed
Wed Keyless SSH with SSM Session Manager 000058 Systems Manager / EC2 IAM Role Connect to private subnet servers securely without key pairs or open port 22 View Lab Completed
Thu Infrastructure as Code (IaC) 000037 CloudFormation / JSON Templates Automate resource provisioning using CloudFormation stacks View Lab Completed
Fri Personal Project: API Gateway Endpoint Personal Project API Gateway / Lambda / CORS Build an HTTP API Gateway endpoint connected to the Lambda backend _ In Progress

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Automated EC2 Shutdown & Slack Alert Notifications View Evidence
Multi-Source Cloud Monitoring via Grafana Dashboards View Evidence
Secure Access via Systems Manager Session Manager View Evidence
Resource Automation using AWS CloudFormation Stacks View Evidence
Personal Project - API Gateway Lambda Integration
1 / 8

3. Topics Covered

  • Serverless Operations Automation:
    • Wrote Lambda automation scripts with IAM execution roles to discover and stop idle servers.
    • Integrated Amazon EventBridge rules to trigger Lambda executions on fixed schedules.
    • Connected an incoming webhook URL to post execution logs to a Slack channel.
  • Custom Monitoring Dashboards (Grafana):
    • Launched a host VM, installed Grafana, and configured an IAM Instance Profile to query metrics.
    • Built custom monitoring dashboards aggregating key metrics like CPU and disk I/O.
  • Systems Manager Session Manager:
    • Wrote IAM Roles containing AWS-managed policies for Systems Manager.
    • Established console-based shell sessions to eliminate standard SSH keys and port 22 vulnerabilities.
  • AWS CloudFormation (IaC):
    • Provisioned resource configurations using JSON-based templates.
    • Acquired understanding of the Stack lifecycle: creates, updates, drift detections, and deletion procedures.
  • Personal Project API Gateway Integration:
    • Created an Amazon API Gateway REST API with a /visitor route pointing to the Lambda counter.
    • Configured Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) rules to accept incoming web calls.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Decreased operating costs by shutting down servers automatically after hours.
  • Standardized monitoring dashboards on Grafana using cloud data sources.
  • Hardened administrative connections on running servers using SSM Session Manager.
  • Successfully provisioned multi-tier networks using AWS CloudFormation.
  • Completed the serverless backend pipeline: API Gateway -> Lambda -> DynamoDB.
END OF WEEK 6

item.title: Week 7 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 2 & 3 Topic: Security - IAM Permission Boundaries, Security Hub, WAF, and IAM Auditing

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 7

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon IAM Permission Boundaries 000030 IAM / Permission Boundary Enforce maximum permission boundaries for delegation accounts View Lab Completed
Tue Restricted IAM Roles via Conditions 000044 IAM / STS / Condition Keys Enforce IP, timeline, and security constraints on AssumeRole calls View Lab Completed
Wed AWS Security Hub Compliance 000018 Security Hub / GuardDuty Enable Security Hub and review compliance scores View Lab Completed
Thu Web Protection via AWS WAF 000026 AWS WAF / Application Load Balancer Protect servers from SQL injection and DDoS attacks using Web ACLs View Lab Completed
Fri Personal Project: Access Audit Personal Project IAM / Lambda Resource Policy Apply Least Privilege to Portfolio IAM roles and audit public access paths _ Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Restricted Delegation via IAM Permission Boundaries View Evidence
Secure AssumeRole Actions with Conditional Keys View Evidence
Automated Security Audits in AWS Security Hub
Hardening Web Endpoints using AWS WAF Rules View Evidence
Personal Project - Serverless Security Audits
1 / 6

3. Topics Covered

  • IAM Permission Boundaries:
    • Wrote boundary policies to limit the maximum permissions delegated administrators can assign.
    • Validated authorization rules on test accounts to ensure privilege escalation was blocked.
  • AssumeRole Condition Policy:
    • Wrote conditional keys in IAM Trust Relationships to limit Role assumption.
    • Configured filters restricting access to corporate IP blocks and active business hours.
  • AWS Security Hub Assessments:
    • Activated Security Hub to run automated security compliance scans against target cloud accounts.
    • Reviewed score breakdowns against standard security frameworks, including CIS and AWS Foundations.
  • AWS WAF Web Application Firewalls:
    • Created Web Access Control Lists (Web ACLs) and attached them to Application Load Balancers.
    • Configured Managed Rulesets to block common exploits, including Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and SQL injection.
  • Personal Project Access Audit:
    • Applied the Principle of Least Privilege to the portfolio Lambda execution roles.
    • Removed unnecessary wildcard resource access permissions ("Resource": "*") from the custom DynamoDB policies.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Blocked privilege escalation attacks using IAM Permission Boundaries.
  • Enforced context-aware authentication rules on IAM AssumeRole actions.
  • Identified security vulnerabilities on AWS accounts using automated compliance scanners.
  • Mitigated common web exploits on public-facing workloads using AWS WAF rules.
  • Audited serverless resource authorization policies on the personal project.
END OF WEEK 7

item.title: Week 8 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 2 & 3 Topic: Reliability & Performance - AWS Backup, SSL/HTTPS, Docker, and Amazon ECS

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 8

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Automated Backup Vaults 000013 AWS Backup / Recovery Plans Configure Backup Vaults and define automated snapshot schedules View Lab Completed
Tue SSL/HTTPS Web Security 000082 CloudFront / Route 53 / ACM Provision TLS/SSL certificates and enforce global HTTPS connections View Lab Completed
Wed Container Packaging with Docker 000015 Dockerfile / Amazon ECR Package a web app as a container image and push it to Amazon ECR View Lab Completed
Thu Orchestrating with Amazon ECS Fargate 000016 Amazon ECS / Task Definitions Deploy containerized workloads on a serverless Fargate cluster View Lab Completed
Fri Personal Project: Production Vue.js build Personal Project Vue.js / TypeScript / Vite Integrate the backend visitor counter into the Vue portfolio and run a production build - Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Centralized Disaster Recovery via AWS Backup Vaults View Evidence
SSL/TLS Certificate Provisioning using ACM View Evidence
Container Image Packaging and Storage on ECR View Evidence
Orchestrating Serverless Containers on Amazon ECS View Evidence
Personal Project - Visitor Counter Integration & Build
1 / 5

3. Topics Covered

  • AWS Backup Disaster Recovery:
    • Configured automated snapshot schedules and lifecycle policies using centralized Backup Plans.
    • Created secure Backup Vaults to store system-wide backups.
  • SSL/TLS ACM Integration:
    • Requested public SSL certificates using AWS Certificate Manager (ACM).
    • Configured secure HTTPS redirection rules on an Application Load Balancer.
  • Docker Containerization:
    • Wrote multi-stage Dockerfiles to optimize image footprint.
    • Created secure private repositories in Amazon Elastic Container Registry (ECR) and pushed container images using the docker CLI.
  • Amazon ECS (Elastic Container Service):
    • Explored container orchestration models, focusing on Task Definitions, Services, and Clusters.
    • Deployed containerized applications on serverless AWS Fargate compute.
  • Personal Project Vue/Vite Build:
    • Modified the portfolio UI source code to send HTTP requests to the API Gateway /visitor endpoint.
    • Verified real-time counter updates on the Vue frontend.
    • Ran a production build with Vite to output the optimized static build directory (dist/).

4. Key Takeaways

  • Automated backup schedules for disaster recovery.
  • Configured end-to-end TLS/SSL encryption.
  • Packaged web applications into Docker container images.
  • Deployed containerized applications on AWS Fargate.
  • Compiled the Vue frontend to optimized static files.
END OF WEEK 8

item.title: Week 9 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 4, 5, 6 & 7 Topic: Serverless Part 1 - S3 & CloudFront Deployment, CI/CD, and AWS SAM

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 9

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Serverless CI/CD Pipeline 000084 AWS CodePipeline / CodeBuild Build a CI/CD pipeline to automate serverless application deployments View Lab Completed
Tue Event-Driven Storage Lambda 000078 S3 Events / AWS Lambda Configure Lambda functions to process data from S3 and update DynamoDB View Lab Completed
Wed Frontend API Integration 000079 API Gateway / CORS / JS Client Build frontend interfaces to send AJAX queries to backend APIs View Lab Completed
Thu AWS SAM Deployments 000080 AWS SAM / CloudFormation Define and deploy serverless architectures using AWS SAM templates View Lab Completed
Fri Personal Project: S3 & CloudFront Personal Project S3 / Amazon CloudFront Upload static assets to S3 and configure a CloudFront CDN distribution - Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Automated Deployments with AWS CodePipeline View Evidence
Event-Driven Data Processing via Lambda View Evidence
Safe Cross-Origin Resource Sharing on APIs View Evidence
Declarative Serverless IaC using AWS SAM View Evidence
Personal Project - CloudFront Distribution Hosting
1 / 5

3. Topics Covered

  • Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment:
    • Created multi-stage deployment pipelines using AWS CodePipeline.
    • Configured AWS CodeBuild stages to build, test, and package serverless code automatically on git push events.
  • Event-Driven Serverless Workloads:
    • Connected S3 object creation triggers (ObjectCreated:Put) to trigger a downstream Lambda function.
    • Wrote data-parsing scripts to store file metadata inside Amazon DynamoDB.
  • AWS Serverless Application Model (SAM):
    • Used AWS SAM templates to model serverless applications (APIs, Lambdas, and Databases) as declarative YAML.
    • Built and deployed SAM architectures using the sam build and sam deploy CLI commands.
  • Personal Project CDN Hosting Integration:
    • Uploaded the optimized static build directory (dist/) to the portfolio S3 bucket.
    • Created an Amazon CloudFront distribution pointing to the S3 bucket to cache portfolio assets globally.
    • Verified global secure HTTPS access via the CloudFront domain:
      • https://ddusufgoys11.cloudfront.net

4. Key Takeaways

  • Constructed automated CI/CD pipelines for serverless applications.
  • Deployed event-driven microservices triggered by file uploads.
  • Configured CORS policies to secure web client API requests.
  • Deployed multi-service serverless applications using AWS SAM.
  • Hosted the portfolio static website globally via Amazon CloudFront CDN.
END OF WEEK 9

item.title: Week 10 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 4, 5, 6 & 7 Topic: Serverless Part 2 - Cognito, SQS/SNS, Step Functions, and CloudFront Error Handling

Note: Detailed contents are updated upon completing each lab practice.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 10

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Cognito User Authentication 000081 Amazon Cognito / JWT Tokens Configure User Pools to secure serverless endpoints View Lab Completed
Tue Asynchronous Message Queues 000083 Amazon SQS / Amazon SNS Build loosely coupled architectures using messaging queues View Lab Completed
Wed Distributed Tracing with X-Ray 000085 AWS X-Ray / CloudWatch Metrics Debug serverless request latency and trace system bottlenecks View Lab Completed
Thu AWS Step Functions Workflows 000047 Step Functions / State Machines Orchestrate complex, multi-step workflows using State Machines View Lab Completed
Fri Personal Project: SPA Refresh Fix Personal Project CloudFront Error Pages / GitHub Actions Fix SPA route refresh errors on CloudFront and set up CI/CD - Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
Secure User Registrations using Amazon Cognito Pools View Evidence
Loosely Coupled Systems via Amazon SQS and SNS View Evidence
Distributed Application Tracing with AWS X-Ray View Evidence
Multi-Step Process Coordination with Step Functions View Evidence
Personal Project - SPA Routing & Pipeline Integration
1 / 8

3. Topics Covered

  • User Identity Management (Cognito):
    • Created Cognito User Pools to securely handle registration, sign-in, and account confirmation.
    • Integrated JWT (JSON Web Tokens) validation to secure backend REST endpoints.
  • Asynchronous Message Processing:
    • Built decoupled, fault-tolerant messaging channels using SQS (Simple Queue Service) and SNS (Simple Notification Service).
    • Implemented fan-out event architectures to trigger parallel backend processes.
  • Distributed App Performance (AWS X-Ray):
    • Wrote X-Ray instrumentation libraries to trace API latencies and downstream database request times.
  • Process Coordination (AWS Step Functions):
    • Orchestrated complex, multi-step business logic using ASL (Amazon States Language) State Machines.
  • Personal Project SPA Route Fixes:
    • Solved the common Single Page Application (SPA) refresh issue where reloading nested routes returned AccessDenied errors from S3.
    • Configured Custom Error Pages on the CloudFront distribution to redirect 403/404 response codes to /index.html with a 200 OK status.
    • Configured a GitHub Actions workflow to automate building and deploying the Vue client to S3 on git pushes.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Standardized secure user authentication flows on serverless APIs.
  • Decoupled messaging backends to absorb sudden load spikes.
  • Traced system latency bottlenecks using AWS X-Ray.
  • Deployed state-machine workflows to coordinate multi-service tasks.
  • Fixed SPA route refresh errors on CloudFront and set up automated CI/CD.
END OF WEEK 10

item.title: Week 11 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 4, 5, 6 & 7 Topic: Architecture Optimization, End-to-End Testing, and Workshop Documentation

Note: This week focused on optimizing the personal portfolio, testing system-wide flows, and writing detailed workshop documentation.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 11

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Designing Portfolio Architecture Personal Project Draw.io / CloudFront / S3 / Lambda Draw the AWS architecture diagram representing actual deployed resources - Completed
Tue System-wide End-to-End Testing Personal Project CloudFront / API Gateway / DynamoDB Validate user client queries, Lambda triggers, and DynamoDB database updates - Completed
Wed Drafting Workshop Docs (Part 1) Personal Project Markdown Docs / Architecture Document the project overview, prerequisites, architecture, and deployment steps - Completed
Thu Drafting Workshop Docs (Part 2) Personal Project Markdown Docs / CloudWatch / Cleanup Document end-to-end testing, logging checks, and clean-up commands - Completed
Fri Audit and Evidence Gathering Personal Project S3 Buckets / CloudWatch Logs Gather screenshots and evidence of running services for the final report - Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
AWS Cloud Portfolio Architecture Diagram
Public Website Running via CloudFront URL
Compiled Production Assets in S3 Bucket
Live API Gateway /visitor Route Execution
1 / 2
Serverless Counter Lambda Logic
Structural Workshop Documentation
1 / 3

3. Topics Covered

  • Designing the Portfolio Architecture Diagram:
    • Designed a clean architecture diagram using Draw.io to represent actual deployed cloud services.
    • Excluded Route 53 from the diagram as the project uses the default CloudFront HTTPS domain instead of a custom domain.
    • Represented actual traffic paths: User -> CloudFront CDN -> S3 (Static Assets) and client AJAX -> API Gateway -> Lambda -> DynamoDB.
  • System-wide End-to-End Testing:
    • Accessed the live site via the CloudFront URL and verified fast, secure asset loads over HTTPS.
    • Verified that reloading pages did not return S3 AccessDenied errors.
    • Tested the /visitor endpoint directly and verified that DB count records incremented properly.
  • Drafting Technical Workshop Documentation:
    • Drafted the Overview section describing project goals and benefits.
    • Drafted the Prerequisites section listing AWS accounts, Git, Node.js, and CLI dependencies.
    • Drafted the Architecture section describing S3 bucket setups and database tables.
    • Drafted the Deployment section detailing Vite builds and S3 asset uploads.
    • Drafted the Testing, Monitoring, and Clean-Up sections to guide users through testing flows, inspecting Lambda execution logs in CloudWatch, and cleaning up resources to prevent unexpected charges.
  • Review and Standardization:
    • Ensured consistent terminology throughout the worklog, clarifying that the S3 bucket hosts “compiled static assets” rather than the raw application “source code.”
    • Audited project links:
      • GitHub repository: https://github.com/tronghieu1201/portfolio
      • GitHub Pages backup: https://tronghieu1201.github.io/portfolio/
      • Live CloudFront domain: https://ddusufgoys11.cloudfront.net

4. Key Takeaways

  • Mapped out the actual deployed resource layout using Draw.io.
  • Validated secure, fast global content delivery via CloudFront.
  • Verified that client API queries update the DynamoDB database.
  • Drafted detailed step-by-step workshop deployment documentation.
  • Organized all necessary system screenshots and evidence for the final report.
END OF WEEK 11

item.title: Week 12 - Worklog
item.description:
Module 4, 5, 6 & 7 Topic: Internship Summary, Knowledge Consolidation, and Final Report Polishing

Note: This final week focused on reviewing my 12-week learning journey, compiling my technical achievements, documenting lessons learned, and polishing the final report.

1. Plan and Deliverables in Week 12

Day Task Description Lab ID Related Service / Topic Primary Objective Reference Status
Mon Reviewing Weeks 1-4 Achievements Summary IAM / VPC / EC2 / S3 / RDS / CloudWatch Review fundamental cloud networking, compute, storage, and databases - Completed
Tue Reviewing Weeks 5-8 Achievements Summary IAM Policies / Lambda / DynamoDB / ECS Review serverless functions, security boundaries, and container orchestration - Completed
Wed Reviewing Weeks 9-11 Achievements Summary S3 / CloudFront / SAM / CI/CD Pipelines Review CDN configurations, infrastructure pipelines, and documentation - Completed
Thu Documenting Lessons Learned Summary Region Checks / Costs / Route Refresh Document common issues, bug resolutions, and cost management practices - Completed
Fri Finalizing the Internship Report Summary Worklog Polishing / Final Submission Review the entire report to ensure weeks are logically linked and clear - Completed

2. Evidence Gallery

Requirement Proof
12-Week Structured Internship Worklog Summary View Evidence
personal Project Serverless Architecture Deployment View Evidence
Consolidating Cloud Knowledge and Best Practices View Evidence

3. Topics Covered

  • Reviewing Fundamental AWS Cloud Topologies (Weeks 1-4):
    • Consolidated my understanding of AWS accounts, Free Tier limits, Billing Dashboards, and IAM cost alerts (Budgets).
    • Reviewed core networking concepts, including custom VPCs, public/private subnets, Internet Gateways, and security groups.
    • Reviewed compute deployments (EC2, Lightsail, Auto Scaling) and relational databases (RDS MySQL) in private subnets.
  • Reviewing Advanced Security & Compute Topologies (Weeks 5-8):
    • Reviewed cost management strategies using region and instance-size constraints in IAM policies.
    • Reviewed tagging strategies, S3 security policies, and SSM Session Manager connections.
    • Consolidated container orchestration concepts on serverless compute (Docker & ECS Fargate).
  • Reviewing Serverless Architectures & Deployment Pipelines (Weeks 9-11):
    • Reviewed serverless application structures, API endpoints, NoSQL database tables (DynamoDB), and Lambda counter functions.
    • Consolidated CDN caching topologies (CloudFront) and automated delivery pipelines (GitHub Actions).
  • Consolidating Key Technical Lessons Learned:
    • Region Consistency: Emphasized checking current active Regions, as mismatched Regions cause most service discovery and routing errors.
    • Cost Management: Stressed monitoring the Billing Dashboard closely, especially after deploying EC2, RDS, NAT Gateways, or WAF.
    • SPA Route Refreshing: Documented the CloudFront Custom Error Page redirect fix (index.html) to resolve route refresh errors.
    • Serverless Debugging: Documented using CloudWatch Logs to trace execution errors across API Gateway, Lambda, and DynamoDB.
  • Self-Evaluation and Growth Areas:
    • Strengths: Successfully integrated distributed AWS services into a functional, secure serverless portfolio with automated CI/CD pipelines.
    • Opportunities for Improvement: Acquired basic concepts for advanced services (Cognito, ECS, Step Functions, SQS/SNS) but have not integrated them deeply.
    • Future Goals: Plan to explore advanced CI/CD pipelines, container orchestration, and IaC (Terraform) in future cloud projects.

4. Key Takeaways

  • Summarized all technical deliverables across the 12-week internship.
  • Reviewed the architecture and deployment steps of the personal project.
  • Documented critical troubleshooting steps, including fixing CloudFront route refresh errors.
  • Identified key areas for future professional growth.
  • Completed the final 12-week internship report for submission.
END OF WORKLOG - 12 WEEKS COMPLETED
